data.title: Trump to decide on Iran in two weeks; Tehran reportedly rushing to export oil
data.description:
President Donald Trump will decide within the next two weeks if the U.S. should launch an attack on Iran, pressuring Tehran to the negotiating table for a nuclear deal.

"Based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks," Trump said in a message delivered by White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt.

The deadline comes as the escalating Israel-Iran conflict enters its second week. Israel struck 100 targets in Iran, including nuclear sites, on Thursday. Iran continues to launch missiles and drones at Israel.

British, French, German and E.U. foreign ministers will meet with their Iranian counterpart in Geneva on Friday to discuss the situation. "The president supports diplomatic efforts from our allies that could bring Iran closer to taking his deal," a White House official said.

To note, Trump weighing a potential strike on Iran has caused division in his support base in the U.S. "We can't do this again," said Steve Bannon, a prominent Trump ally. "We'll tear the country apart. We can't have another Iraq."

Eye on oil

Iran is racing to get its oil out into the world, Bloomberg reported, as satellite images show storage tanks at the Kharg Island export terminal are brimming with crude. 

At the same time, Iran has been driving up its crude exports. This likely means that Iran is directing even more crude into the facility to ensure that it can send as much oil as possible to the global market.

Iran ships 90% of its crude exports from the Kharg Island export terminal, according to S&P Global Commodity Insights.

Meanwhile, Israel could face major fuel supply challenges if it fails to repair drone damage to its Haifa plant soon. S&P said the outage could turn Israel into a net oil product importer for its fuel, and surging imports will test the limits of domestic port infrastructure.

data.title: HIVE Digital surpasses 11 EH/s bitcoin hashrate milestone
data.description:
• HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) has surpassed 11 Exahash per second (EH/s) of global Bitcoin mining hashrate, achieving an operational hashrate of 11.4 EH/s.
  • This milestone marks the early and successful completion of Phase 1 (100 MW) of HIVE's Yguazú site in Paraguay - a pivotal step in its mission to scale to 25 EH/s by American Thanksgiving 2025.
  • Over the past five weeks, HIVE has steadily increased its installed hash rate by approximately 1 EH/s per week, reaching 11 EH/s last week.

data.title: LNG emissions could be cut by 60% with existing tech, $100 billion investment needed - IEA
data.description:
Greenhouse gas emissions from the liquefied natural gas (LNG) supply chain could be reduced by over 60% using existing technologies, according to a new report from the International Energy Agency (IEA).

The IEA  estimates that the upfront capital investment needed to implement electrification, methane reduction, and CCUS across all viable facilities in the existing LNG supply chain is just over $100 billion.

Greenhouse gas emissions associated with global LNG supply are roughly 350 million tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent annually.

Around 70% of this is in the form of CO2 emissions that are either combusted or vented; the remaining 30% is methane that escapes unburnt into the atmosphere, the IEA said.

According to the report, when all direct and indirect greenhouse gas emissions are factored in, more than 99% of the LNG consumed in 2024 had lower lifecycle emissions than coal.

Globally, on average, LNG results in about 25% less emissions than coal.

Nevertheless, the report emphasizes that for those making an environmental case for LNG use, comparing it only to coal – the most carbon-intensive fuel – sets the bar too low, "especially given the strong opportunities at hand for improving the emissions performance of LNG supply".

Other cost-effective strategies that could substantially reduce emissions include increasing process efficiency across the supply chain and implementing carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS) at liquefaction facilities to capture the naturally occurring CO2 present in the feed gas.

The global LNG market is dominated by several major companies, with Shell (SHEL), ExxonMobil (XOM), QatarEnergy, TotalEnergies (TTE), and Chevron (CVX) leading the industry in production and export capacity.

data.title: BBC threatens legal action versus Perplexity over alleged use of content to train AI models - report
data.description:
U.K. broadcaster BBC has threatened legal action against AI search engine Perplexity alleging the startup of training its default AI model using BBC content, the Financial Times reported.

In a letter to Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas seen by the news outlet, the British national broadcaster said it could seek an injunction unless the company stops scraping all BBC content, deletes any copies of BBC's material held for developing its AI systems, and provides “a proposal for financial compensation” for the alleged infringement of its intellectual property so far, the report added.

BBC and Perplexity did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Perplexity said BBC's claims were "manipulative and opportunistic" in a statement to the FT, adding that BBC had "a fundamental misunderstanding of technology, the internet and intellectual property law."

Last year in October, the New York Times (NYT) has sent Perplexity — which is backed by major investors such as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) Co-Founder Jeff Bezos — a "cease and desist" notice demanding the company stop using its content.

In the past, several tech companies developing large language models, or LLMs, have faced heat related to alleged use of copyright content.

In December 2023, The New York Times sued Microsoft (MSFT) and OpenAI for copyright infringement, alleging that the tech companies illegally used the newspaper's content to train AI models. In March, the judge had ruled that the core elements of the case could proceed.

Earlier this month, OpenAI appealed an order in a copyright case filed by the New York Times that requires it to save ChatGPT output data indefinitely.

However, some tech giants are paying to license content from online publishers.

data.title: Tesla signs deal for first China grid-scale battery station - report
data.description:
• Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) first grid-scale battery storage project in China, using its Megapack batteries, will be situated in Shanghai, local outlet Yicai reported Friday.
  • The $556.8M (4 billion yuan) deal was signed on Friday between Tesla, China Kangfu International Leasing Co., and the Shanghai municipal government, according to the report.
  • TSLA shares were up 1.3% premarket.

data.title: European markets rise, Eutelsat shares jump 6% following French government capital injection
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.44%

UK retail sales fall sharply in May.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.02%

France (CAC:IND)  +0.79%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.6% on Friday, looking to erase some of the week’s losses, as most sectors traded in green, while oil and gas shares fell.

Eutelsat (OTCPK:EUTLF) shares rose after the European satellite operator announced a 1.35 billion euro ($1.55 billion) capital raise backed by the French state and other shareholders.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 1 basis point at 4.39%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point at 2.51%.

The UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point at 4.52%.

Coming up in the session: No major events lined up.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Stock index futures rise as threat of immediate U.S. strike on Iran averted
data.description:
Stock index futures gained sharply on Friday, as an immediate U.S. strike on Iran seemed to have been averted, while investors weighed the Federal Reserve's rate decision. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.9%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +1.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.8%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.39%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.95%.

President Donald Trump is expected to decide within two weeks on a possible U.S. strike on Iran amid the latter's conflict with Israel. Investors took some relief from this after talks of the U.S. entering the tensions hurt sentiments. 

"Markets are recovering a bit this morning after White House spokeswoman Leavitt last night said that Trump had dictated a message saying that 'based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks,'" Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

That means two weeks of uncertainty for financial markets, but investors are still inclined to see the Middle East conflict as a local, not a global, economic issue, UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Wall Street on Wednesday erased modest gains to end little changed, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said uncertainty stemming from tariffs continued to call for a "wait-and-see" approach to monetary policy actions.

The Fed left its policy rate at 4.25%-4.50% on Wednesday for a fourth straight meeting. 

The U.S. stock market was closed on Thursday to observe Juneteenth, after the NYSE and Nasdaq adopted the day as a new market holiday in 2022.

Turning to the economic side, the June Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index will land before the opening bell and is expected to come in at -1.7.

The May U.S. Leading Index will be released during market hours and is forecasted to come in at -0.1% M/M.

The Fed's balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: IC Group secures two contracts with Global tech leader valued up to $7.8M
Stellantis and Renault urge EU to ease regulations amid rising competition from China's small cars
data.description:
As Chinese automakers expand into Europe, Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) and Renault (OTCPK:RNSDF) are advocating for a new, less-regulated class of small cars with reduced safety features to lower production costs, Reuters reported.

Over the past two months, Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) Chairman John Elkann and Renault CEO Luca de Meo have launched an unusual public campaign urging the European Union to consider the proposal.

Stellantis Chairman John Elkann has suggested that Europe adopt a class of small, affordable vehicles similar to Japan's "kei cars."

"There’s no reason why if Japan has a kei car, which is 40% of the market, Europe should not have an e-car," he said at an event in Turin, echoing similar remarks in a joint editorial with Renault’s de Meo published last month, the report said.

Though de Meo is set to leave Renault in July, the company is expected to maintain its support for the proposal.

The lobbying effort targets the EU’s General Safety Regulations 2 (GSR2), which mandates safety features such as side airbags, sensors detecting whether a driver is falling asleep, lane-crossing warning, and more thorough crash tests, the report added.

China’s BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) launched the Dolphin Surf just a month ago, offering it at a starting price of under 20,000 euros ($23,124). 

Under pressure from rising competition, European automakers are exploring the potential of more affordable vehicles to drive sales and meet CO2 emissions targets, the report said, quoting Flavien Neuvy, auto analyst and head of research at Cetelem.

Matthew Avery, Director of Strategic Development at Euro NCAP, noted that Chinese automakers are entering the European market with vehicles that consistently earn five-star Euro NCAP ratings.

While these ratings aren't legally binding, Avery emphasized that they heavily influence consumer decisions—and many corporate fleets refuse to purchase cars rated below five stars.

data.title: Amazon to invest $233M to strengthen India operations network
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) plans to invest about $233M in its India business to support network expansion and upgrades for faster and more reliable deliveries.

“This investment will enhance processing capacity, improve fulfilment speed, and increase efficiency across the company's operations network that will help Amazon serve customers across India faster and more reliably,” the company said in a release.

As part of the investment, the company will continue work on some initiatives to support its contractors and employees, including rest spots for delivery personnel; financial education and support; scholarships for associates’ children; and healthcare support for truck drivers.

The e-commerce giant also said it is developing new software and tools to help delivery personnel with navigation, safety, and the overall delivery process.
